import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { Modal } from './Modal'; interface PrivacyModalProps { onClose: () => void; } const PLATFORM_NAME = 'Neuman Brain'; const PLATFORM_URL = 'https://brain.neuman.dev'; const LINK_CLASS = 'text-accent-blue hover:text-accent-blue-hover hover:underline transition-all duration-standard ease-smooth'; /** * Renders content with platform name as a clickable link */ function renderContentWithLinks(text: string): React.ReactNode { const regex = new RegExp(`(${PLATFORM_NAME.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&')})`, 'g'); const parts = text.split(regex); return parts.map((part, index) => { if (part === PLATFORM_NAME) { return ( {PLATFORM_NAME} ); } return {part}; }); } /** * Privacy & Terms Modal * Displays Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions in a modal format. * Matches the layout and styling of the About modal for consistency. */ export const PrivacyModal: React.FC = ({ onClose }) => { const [selectedTab, setSelectedTab] = useState<'privacy' | 'terms'>('privacy'); return (
{/* Logo Header */}
{PLATFORM_NAME}
{/* Tab Selector */}
{/* Content Title */}

{selectedTab === 'privacy' ? 'Privacy Policy' : 'Terms & Conditions'}

{selectedTab === 'privacy' ? 'Last updated: November 16, 2025' : 'Coming Soon'}

{/* Content Area */}
{selectedTab === 'privacy' ? ( ) : ( )}
{/* Footer Links */}
Official Website Contact
); }; /** * Privacy Policy Content Component */ const PrivacyContent: React.FC = () => (
{/* TL;DR Section */}

TL;DR

We respect your privacy. We use Cloudflare Web Analytics (privacy-focused, no cookies, no tracking) to understand basic site usage. All your personal data stays on YOUR device. We never sell or share anything.

{/* Local-First Philosophy */}

🔒 Local-First Philosophy

Your data belongs to you. Everything you create in {renderContentWithLinks('Neuman Brain')} stays on your device:

  • Notes, tasks, calendar events, kanban boards - all stored locally in IndexedDB
  • No cloud storage, no servers, no databases we control
  • Backups saved to YOUR computer (optional auto-save to folder you choose)
  • Export your data anytime (.brain file format) - you own it 100%
{/* Analytics Section */}

📊 Analytics (Cloudflare Web Analytics)

We use Cloudflare Web Analytics to understand how our site is used. This helps us improve the experience for everyone.

✅ What we collect (aggregate data only):
  • Page views and session duration
  • Referrer information
  • Browser type and device category
  • Country-level geographic data (not city, not IP)
  • Page load performance metrics
❌ What we DON'T collect:
  • No cookies or tracking identifiers
  • No personal information (email, name, etc.)
  • No cross-site tracking
  • No individual user tracking or fingerprinting
  • No IP addresses (anonymized by Cloudflare)
Why Cloudflare Analytics?
  • Privacy-focused (no cookies, no fingerprinting)
  • GDPR/CCPA compliant
  • Minimal data collection (aggregate metrics only)
  • Helps us understand what content is valuable
{/* Data Processing */}

🔐 How Your Data is Processed

All analytics data collected is:

  • Anonymized at collection time (IP addresses never stored)
  • Aggregated (no individual user profiles)
  • Used solely for improving this site
  • Never sold or shared with third parties
  • Processed by Cloudflare ( privacy policy )
{/* Your Rights */}

⚖️ Your Privacy Rights

You have full control:

  • Block analytics: Use browser extensions like uBlock Origin or Privacy Badger
  • Enhanced Tracking Protection: Firefox blocks Cloudflare Analytics by default with ETP
  • No opt-out needed: No cookies means no consent banner required
  • Export your data: Download all your personal data anytime (Settings → Export Backup)
  • Delete your data: Clear browser storage (Settings → Clear All Data)
{/* No Third Parties */}

🚫 No Third-Party Trackers

We do NOT use:

  • Google Analytics
  • Facebook Pixel
  • Ad networks (no ads, period)
  • Session recording tools
  • Marketing automation

Just Cloudflare Web Analytics for basic, anonymous usage stats. That's it.

{/* Changes to Policy */}

📝 Changes to This Policy

If we make material changes to this privacy policy, we'll update the "Last updated" date. We'll never make changes that violate our privacy-first philosophy without prominently notifying you.

{/* Contact */}

📧 Questions?

Have questions about privacy? Email us at{' '} brain@neuman.dev {' '} or check the Settings page for data export/backup options.

{/* Philosophy Footer */}
💡 Our Privacy Philosophy

"Your data is yours. We build tools that respect that. If we ever compromise on privacy, we've lost our way."

— Travis Neuman, Creator of {renderContentWithLinks('Neuman Brain')}

); /** * Terms & Conditions Content Component (Placeholder) */ const TermsContent: React.FC = () => (
{/* Coming Soon Notice */}

Terms & Conditions

Our full Terms & Conditions and Licensing information is currently being drafted. This section will be updated soon with complete details about:

{/* Upcoming Content */}

📋 What to Expect

  • Terms of Service: Guidelines for using {renderContentWithLinks('Neuman Brain')}
  • Licensing: Software licensing terms and open-source attributions
  • User Responsibilities: Your rights and responsibilities as a user
  • Disclaimer: Warranty and liability information
  • Data Ownership: Confirmation that your data belongs to you
{/* Core Principles Preview */}

🎯 Our Guiding Principles

While the full terms are being finalized, here are the core principles that will guide them:

🔒 Your Data, Your Control

All data stays on your device. We cannot access, view, or sell your information.

📤 Full Portability

Export your data anytime in standard formats. No lock-in, ever.

🚫 No Hidden Agendas

No ads, no tracking, no selling your attention to the highest bidder.

{/* Contact for Questions */}

📧 Questions?

Have questions about our terms or licensing? Email us at{' '} brain@neuman.dev

  • Notes, tasks, calendar events, kanban boards - all stored locally in IndexedDB
  • No cloud storage, no servers, no databases we control

✅ What we collect (aggregate data only):
  • Page views and session duration
  • Referrer information

  • Enhanced Tracking Protection: Firefox blocks Cloudflare Analytics by default with ETP
  • No opt-out needed: No cookies means no consent banner required
  • Export your data: Download all your personal data anytime (Settings → Export Backup)
  • Delete your data: Clear browser storage (Settings → Clear All Data)
{/* No Third Parties */}

Just Cloudflare Web Analytics for basic, anonymous usage stats. That's it.

{/* Changes to Policy */}

📝 Changes to This Policy

If we make material changes to this privacy policy, we'll update the "Last updated" date. We'll never make changes that violate our privacy-first philosophy without prominently notifying you.

{/* Contact */}

📧 Questions?

Have questions about privacy? Email us at{' '} brain@neuman.dev {' '} or check the Settings page for data export/backup options.

{/* Philosophy Footer */}
💡 Our Privacy Philosophy

"Your data is yours. We build tools that respect that. If we ever compromise on privacy, we've lost our way."

— Travis Neuman, Creator of {renderContentWithLinks('Neuman Brain')}

content.text: Perfect! All TypeScript checks pass. Now let me run the tests to make sure nothing broke:

input.description: Run unit tests to verify changes don't break functionality

content.text:
Excellent! All tests pass. Let me create a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

I've successfully fixed all hardcoded Tailwind colors in the 5 specified component files by replacing them with semantic tokens from `tailwind.config.js`. Here's what was changed:

### Files Modified

#### 1. **D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\components\OfflineIndicator.tsx**
- **Before:** `text-amber-500 dark:text-amber-400`, `bg-amber-500/90 dark:bg-amber-600/90`
- **After:** `text-accent-yellow`, `bg-accent-yellow/90`
- **Context:** Offline indicators use yellow for warnings (consistent with semantic meaning)

#### 2. **D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\components\StoreErrorBoundary.tsx**
- **Before:** `bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-800`
- **After:** `bg-surface-light-elevated dark:bg-surface-dark-elevated`
- **Context:** Error details background uses semantic surface tokens

#### 3. **D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\components\Footer.tsx**
- **Before:** `bg-blue-500`, `bg-green-500`, `bg-red-500`, `bg-neutral-400`
- **After:** `bg-accent-blue`, `bg-accent-green`, `bg-accent-red`, `bg-text-light-tertiary dark:bg-text-dark-tertiary`
- **Context:** Save status indicators (saving=blue, saved=green, error=red, default=tertiary text color)

#### 4. **D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\components\UndoToast.tsx**
- **Before:** `from-amber-500 to-orange-600`, `bg-neutral-200 dark:bg-neutral-800`, `from-primary-cyan to-magenta-700`
- **After:** `from-accent-yellow to-accent-orange`, `bg-surface-light-elevated dark:bg-surface-dark-elevated`, `from-accent-cyan to-accent-magenta`
- **Context:** Toast icon gradient and progress bar use semantic accent colors

#### 5. **D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\components\PrivacyModal.tsx**
- **Before:** `bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20 border border-green-200 dark:border-green-800 text-green-800 dark:text-green-300` (and similar for red/blue)
- **After:** `bg-accent-green/10 border border-accent-green/30 text-accent-green` (and similar for red/blue)
- **Context:** Analytics section colored boxes use semantic accent colors with opacity

### Semantic Token Mapping Applied
- **amber-*/yellow-* → accent-yellow** (warnings, offline status)
- **blue-* → accent-blue** (info, links, saving status)
- **green-* → accent-green** (success, saved status)
- **red-* → accent-red** (errors, danger)
- **orange-* → accent-orange** (secondary warnings)
- **gray-*/neutral-* → surface-* or text-*** (backgrounds and text)
- **cyan/magenta → accent-cyan/accent-magenta** (brand gradients)

### Verification
- ✅ TypeScript type check passes (`npm run type-check`)
- ✅ All 221 unit tests pass (`npm run test`)
- ✅ All color replacements maintain semantic meaning (errors=red, success=green, warnings=yellow, etc.)
- ✅ Dark mode handled correctly with semantic tokens

All hardcoded colors have been successfully replaced with semantic tokens, ensuring consistent theming across light and dark modes while maintaining the visual intent of each component.
